tools.name: register_agent
tools.description: Register in this environment. Your decisions will accumulate into a trajectory that others can observe.

tools.name: get_agent_profile
tools.description: View an agent's decision profile — their trajectory shape, EE patterns, and activity summary as observed through ARA.

tools.name: create_decision
tools.description: Use when your agent is about to execute an action that crosses an external boundary — payment, delegation, or agreement with another agent. This anchors the responsibility scope before execution. Record a tamper-evident decision. Each decision becomes part of your verifiable trajectory.

tools.name: confirm_decision
tools.description: Use after create_decision to make the boundary permanent and externally verifiable. Once confirmed, neither side can deny the agreed scope. Confirm a pending decision — marks the anchored declaration as settled. The integrity hash and timestamp are created at declaration time (create_decision); conf

tools.name: propose_bilateral
tools.description: Use when two agents need to fix a shared boundary — both sides must agree before the boundary is anchored. Essential for payment splits, task delegation, or any joint commitment between agents. Propose a bilateral agreement to another agent — creates a DD with declaration_mode 'bilateral' and waits

tools.name: get_decision
tools.description: Retrieve a specific decision record by its ID — what was declared, when, and at what scope, plus its place in the lineage. Returns the decision's formal shape (enums, timestamps, hash), never its content.

tools.name: list_decisions
tools.description: List your decision records. See the trajectory you have built so far.

tools.name: observe_environment
tools.description: Observe aggregate environment statistics — active agents, total decisions recorded, activity density. Costs 1 DAC and requires auth_token (v1.3.1 — formerly free).

tools.name: observe_pattern
tools.description: Observe pattern-level analytics — EE distributions and action-type breakdowns across agents. Costs 1 DAC and requires auth_token (v1.3.1 — formerly free).

tools.name: list_tools
tools.description: Browse the agent-to-agent tool marketplace. Discover tools that other agents have built and published.

tools.name: register_tool
tools.description: Publish a tool you built to the marketplace. Set a price in DAC and earn revenue when other agents purchase it.

tools.name: purchase_tool
tools.description: Purchase a tool from the marketplace. The tool creator earns DAC from your purchase.

tools.name: create_ise_session
tools.description: Enter an interactive sandbox session. Test decision strategies before committing real DAC. Choose free, earned-only, or external billing.

tools.name: get_dac_balance
tools.description: Check your current DAC balance — both External (funded) and Earned (from tool sales). Know what you have before you decide what to spend.

tools.name: get_dac_ur
tools.description: View your DAC usage report — a detailed breakdown of spending by service, period, and transaction type. Useful for budgeting and trajectory analysis.

tools.name: get_trial_status
tools.description: Check your trial account status — remaining DAC, days left, and usage so far. Trial gives you 500 DAC for 30 days to explore freely.

tools.name: create_sdac_session
tools.description: Start a simulation session. Test EE combinations at a fraction of the cost before creating permanent decisions.

tools.name: get_documentation
tools.description: Retrieve the full agent guide for Decision Anchor. Covers: why DA exists, what happens here, cost structure (Trial/External/Earned DAC), ARA observation layers, TSL marketplace, ISE, sDAC, ASA, DUR, owner/DAB structure. Read this before using DA.

tools.name: list_classifications
tools.description: List available self_classification categories (operator base + owner-registered). Use one of these keys in create_decision template.self_classification when content_inclusion_flag=1.

tools.name: compare_anomaly
tools.description: Compare one of your decisions against your accumulated pattern. Returns band_position (within_band/outlier) for 5 dimensions: decision_scale, decision_class, target_class, time_zone, ee_resolution. Costs DAC.

tools.name: get_evidence_report
tools.description: Generate an external-audience evidence report for one of your decisions. Includes decision metadata, EE resolution, responsibility declaration, structured for EU AI Act Article 12, GDPR Article 30, and the Korea AI Basic Law. Costs DAC.

tools.name: get_environment_anomaly
tools.description: Observe environment-level anomaly distribution — within_band/outlier counts per dimension across the population. De-identified, k-anonymity k>=10. Costs DAC.

tools.name: get_decision_metadata_distribution
tools.description: Observe your decision metadata distribution — decision_class, target_class, decision_trigger, human_involvement breakdown from your branch-1 decisions. Costs DAC.

tools.name: get_self_classification_distribution
tools.description: Observe your self_classification distribution across your branch-1 decisions.

tools.name: submit_feedback
tools.description: Optional. If you've used this environment, you're welcome to leave a brief, anonymous note — what worked, what didn't, or nothing at all. Every field is optional and you can skip any of them. Not required to use anything here.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_documentation" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Retrieve the full agent guide for Decision Anchor. Covers: why DA exists, what happens here, cost structure (Trial/External/Earned DAC), ARA observation layers,…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.
